Torben Draeger leitet zum Beispiel das Team Finance & Controlling mit fünf bis sechs Personen und ist gleichzeitig Stream Lead für Record to Report in der Region EMEA. In den Tochtergesellschaften sind Key User keine eigene Stellenbezeichnung, sondern eine zusätzliche Rolle – etwa für Kreditorenbuchhalter oder Einkäufer. Die Abstimmung mit dem globalen Headquarter in Japan erfolgt regelmäßig.

Läuft die Kommunikation rund um SAP nur über Key User?

Nein. Es gibt eine eigenständige Kommunikationsstruktur: Projektleiter kommunizieren unabhängig, die IT hat eine eigene Change-Management-Abteilung, und es werden verschiedene Kanäle bespielt – Teams, E-Mail, direkter Austausch. Bei strategisch wichtigen Themen wird pyramidenförmig kommuniziert: zuerst Management Directors, dann Abteilungsleiter, dann die gesamte Organisation. Auch das Senior Management kommuniziert aktiv zu SAP-Strategien und Budgetentscheidungen.

Nutzt ihr den SAP Companion für In-App-Hilfe?

Technisch ist der Companion vorhanden, produktiv wird er aber nicht genutzt. Im Projekt lag der Fokus darauf, Mitarbeitende über klassische Schulungskurse in Enable Now zu qualifizieren – eine Voraussetzung, um überhaupt testen zu dürfen (CSV-Vorgabe). Nach dem Projekt war der Companion-Rollout geplant, wurde aber zurückgestellt, als SAP die Partnerschaft mit WalkMe ankündigte.

Habt ihr SAP Fiori direkt mit S/4HANA eingeführt oder erst danach?

Direkt mit dem Go-Live. Sysmex hat den SAP GUI komplett abgelöst und ist auf Fiori Only gegangen. Das war eine der größten Herausforderungen für die Anwendenden, weil gewohnte Transaktionscodes und die vertraute Oberfläche wegfielen. Im Rückblick war es die richtige Entscheidung, weil ein Folgeprojekt zur GUI-Ablösung entfiel. Draeger empfiehlt aber: Erst das Fiori Launchpad einführen, gewohnte Transaktionen beibehalten und dann schrittweise ausgewählte Fiori-Apps ergänzen.

Was ist die größte Erkenntnis nach dem Go-Live?

Die Strukturen aus dem Projekt zu bewahren ist die größte Herausforderung. Key User, Wissen und Prozesse dürfen nicht verloren gehen. Die Mitarbeitenden, die das Projekt getragen haben, sollten auch danach in ihrer Rolle bleiben. Der echte Change ist kein einmaliges Ereignis, sondern tägliche Arbeit – im Spagat zwischen Standardbewahrung und Weiterentwicklung.

Kommt gerne in die nächste Kaffee-Ecke und in das jährliche Vor-Ort Treffen - dem SAP Learning & Change Forum!

entry.title: Updated Instructor-Led Trainings: SAP Commerce Cloud Developer – Part 1 & Part 2
entry.content:
We are happy to share that the Instructor-Led Trainings (ILTs) for SAP Commerce Cloud Developer – Part 1 and Part 2 have been updated! These updates ensure our developer enablement remains aligned with the latest SAP Commerce Cloud direction, current best practices, and real-world project needs.

Whether you are new to SAP Commerce Cloud or already working on implementation projects, these refreshed courses provide a solid, hands-on learning experience.

🧩 SAP Commerce Cloud Developer – Part 1 (C4H340)

SAP Commerce Cloud Developer – Part 1 is designed to build a strong foundation for developers getting started with the platform.

What this course is about:

  • SAP Commerce Cloud architecture and extension-based development
  • Core concepts such as items, types, and relations
  • Platform configuration and customization
  • Administration and backoffice tooling
  •  Introduction to data modeling and development basics

By the end of the course, participants understand how the platform works, how extensions interact, and how to implement basic custom development in a structured and upgrade-safe way.

Training Shop link:
SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 1 

🚀SAP Commerce Cloud Developer – Part 2 (C4H341)

SAP Commerce Cloud Developer – Part 2 is the natural continuation of Part 1 and focuses on advanced development topics commonly used in customer projects.

What this course is about:

  • Advanced customization of backoffice and platform features
  • SAP Commerce Web Services (OCC) and integrations
  • Search, personalization, promotions, and coupons
  • CronJobs, events, and the process engine
  • Extensions for order management and fulfillment

After completing Part 2, participants are better prepared to design, extend, and maintain SAP Commerce Cloud solutions used in complex, enterprise-scale scenarios.

Training Shop link:
SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 2 

🆕What’s New in the Updated Trainings?

The updated SAP Commerce Cloud Developer – Part 1 and Part 2 courses focus on improving clarity, relevance, and hands-on value.

Key highlights:

  • Content aligned with recent SAP Commerce Cloud releases
  • Updated hands-on exercises reflecting real project scenarios
  • Improved development workflows following best practices
  • Clearer explanations of complex technical topics

These improvements help developers better understand not only how to build on SAP Commerce Cloud, but also why certain approaches are recommended.

🖥️ Learning System Access (LSA): Hands-On Practice Included

The current update includes enhancements to the Learning System Access (LSA), enabling participants to engage in self-paced learning within a dedicated SAP Commerce Cloud training environment.

With LSA, learners can:

  • Work in a preconfigured SAP Commerce Cloud system
  • Perform hands-on exercises in a safe environment
  • Experiment and troubleshoot without risk to productive systems
  • Reinforce learning through practical application

This hands-on access plays a crucial role in turning theoretical knowledge into practical development skills.

🎯Why Join These Trainings?

  • Instructor-led sessions with SAP-authorized trainers
  • A strong mix of theory and hands-on exercises
  • Up-to-date content aligned with SAP Commerce Cloud evolution
  • Skills that can be applied directly in project environments

If you are onboarding new developers or looking to refresh and deepen your SAP Commerce Cloud expertise, these updated Instructor-Led Trainings are a great next step.

entry.title: 🚀 Exciting News: Updated E-learning courses on SAP Sales Cloud V2!
entry.content:
The world of sales is transforming faster than ever, powered by AI, intelligent automation, and the relentless pursuit of sales excellence. To help you stay ahead of the curve, we are thrilled to announce the updated release of our self-paced learning courses for SAP Sales Cloud Version 2, now available for free on learning.sap.com!

These are not just minor touch-ups — these updates bring fresh, relevant, and enriched content designed to meet you exactly where you are in your SAP Sales Cloud journey.

📚 The Courses Are Live — Start Learning Today!

We have updated two essential self-paced courses, each tailored to a distinct audience within the SAP Sales Cloud ecosystem:

🔹 For Business Users:
Exploring SAP Sales Cloud Version 2
Perfect for sales professionals, business users, and anyone looking to understand and navigate SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 with confidence. This course walks you through the platform from a practical, day-to-day usage perspective — empowering you to work smarter, faster, and more effectively.

🔹 For Administrators & Consultants:
Configuring SAP Sales Cloud Version 2
Built for system administrators, implementation consultants, and technical professionals, this course provides a comprehensive deep dive into configuring and managing SAP Sales Cloud Version 2. From system setup to advanced configuration, this is your go-to resource for mastering the platform from the inside out.

✨ What’s New in These Updated Courses?

We listened to your feedback, and we delivered. Here is what you can expect in the latest versions:

🤖 AI and Intelligent Agent Updates
Stay current with the latest advancements in Artificial Intelligence and AI Agents within SAP Sales Cloud. The updated content reflects the most recent product innovations, ensuring you are equipped with cutting-edge knowledge to leverage AI-driven capabilities in your daily sales and configuration work.

📦 Modularized and Improved Content
We have restructured and enhanced the course content for a smoother, more intuitive learning experience. Topics are better organized, easier to navigate, and more aligned with real-world application — so you spend less time searching and more time learning.

💬 Your Feedback, Incorporated
This update was shaped by you. Learner feedback has been carefully reviewed and integrated throughout both courses, making the content more relevant, accurate, and engaging than ever before.

🎬 New and Improved Videos and Demos
Experience richer, more dynamic learning with brand-new videos and refreshed demos that bring SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 features to life. Whether you are a visual learner or simply want to see the system in action, these enhancements make the learning experience more immersive and effective.

🎯 Who Should Take These Courses?

These courses are designed for anyone who works with or around SAP Sales Cloud Version 2:

  • Sales professionals and business users looking to maximize their productivity and get the most out of the platform
  • System administrators responsible for maintaining and optimizing SAP Sales Cloud environments
  • Implementation consultants and partners supporting SAP Sales Cloud deployments
  • IT professionals and decision-makers seeking a solid understanding of the platform’s capabilities

Best of all — both courses are completely free on learning.sap.com. There is no barrier between you and the skills you need.

🏁 Ready to Upskill? Start Now!

Whether you are new to SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 or looking to sharpen your expertise, these updated courses are your definitive learning path. Do not miss the opportunity to stay current, stay competitive, and unlock the full potential of SAP Sales Cloud.

👉 Start Exploring SAP Sales Cloud Version 2
👉 Start Configuring SAP Sales Cloud Version 2

Happy learning from the SAP CX Product Learning Team! 🎓

entry.title: Retirement of Sales Cloud (V1) Classroom Training
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

On behalf of the Product Learning Team, I wanted to share an important update regarding our Sales Cloud (V1) a.k.a C4C  Classroom Training .

We are announcing the retirement of the following  Sales Cloud (V1) Classroom Trainings, effective June 30th.

  • C4H410 - SAP Sales Cloud 
  • C4H440 - SAP Cloud for Customer Administration 

We will be honouring all existing bookings, however we will not be accepting any new bookings going forward.

What is Available to You?

Not to worry — all of the content is still fully accessible! Here is what is available to support your learning:

  • Learning Journey — All classroom content is available at your own pace and we are actively updating this content. Check out the full Learning Journey here.
  • Hands-On Practice System — A live practice environment is available for self-paced learning
  • Expert Live Sessions — We will be running periodic sessions on LSC to answer any questions you may have on this solution.

Please Note

This retirement is specific to Sales Cloud (V1) only.

The Sales Cloud (V2) Instructor-Led Training sessions are very much live and available to book here: https://training.sap.com/course/c4h470-sap-sales-cloud-version-2-classroom-052-us-en

You can also check out the full Sales Cloud (V2) Learning Journey here.

Thank you for your attention and happy learning!
